info.title: Phone Numbers - Voice
info.summary: View and bulk provision voice features on phone numbers.
info.description: View and bulk provision voice features on phone numbers.

tags.name: Voice Phone Numbers
tags.description: View and search phone number(s) and the current state of their provisioned voice features.

tags.name: Bulk Voice Phone Number Provisioning
tags.description: Create and view the current status of async bulk provisioning actions.

get.summary: List Phone Numbers with Voice Features
get.description: Returns the current state of voice features and settings provisioned for a paged set of phone numbers.

patch.description: Add, remove, or update multiple TN overrides at once.

post.description: Search and filter phone numbers by the phone numbers themselves or the current state of their provisioned voice features and settings. Returns the current state of voice features and settings provisioned for a paged set of phone numbers.

get.summary: Get Phone Number with Voice Features
get.description: Returns the current state of voice features and settings provisioned for a phone number.

post.description: Create request for a bulk provisioning action to be performed on voice phone numbers. Requests that cannot be completed immediately will be processed asynchronously and a reference ID will be returned to check the status of the request at any time.

get.summary: Get bulk provision request details and current status.
get.description: Get details of an async bulk provisioning action performed on voice phone numbers.

Basic.description:
Basic authentication is a simple authentication scheme built into the HTTP protocol. To use it, send your HTTP requests with an Authorization header that contains the word Basic followed by a space and a base64-encoded string `username:password`.
Example: `Authorization: Basic ZGVtbZpwQDU1dzByZA==`

Bearer.description:
Bearer authentication (also called token authentication) is an HTTP authentication scheme that involves security tokens called bearer tokens. The name “Bearer authentication” can be understood as “give access to the bearer of this token.” The client must send this token in the Authorization header when making requests to protected resources.

Example: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
